# ClawHub

> ClawHub is the public registry for OpenClaw skills, plugins, and packages. Use it to discover, inspect, install, publish, update, and moderate agent-facing artifacts.

ClawHub is the registry layer for OpenClaw. Use native `openclaw` commands when installing skills or plugins into an OpenClaw workspace. Use the separate `clawhub` CLI for authenticated registry workflows such as login, publishing, sync, delete/undelete, transfer, package inspection, and moderation-aware diagnostics.

ClawHub pages and APIs expose live registry state: owner, slug or package name, versions, tags, changelogs, source attribution, files, downloads, stars, install counts, scan summaries, moderation status, and canonical URLs. Security audit results are useful trust signals, not guarantees. Before recommending an install, inspect the artifact page, version, source, changelog, required credentials, and scan state.

This file is a map for language models and agents. It is not an access-control policy. For crawler rules, use `robots.txt`.

## Primary

- [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai): Public registry app for browsing skills, plugin/package records, and publishers.
- [OpenClaw ClawHub docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub): Canonical public documentation for ClawHub users, publishers, API clients, and operators.
- [OpenClaw docs llms.txt](https://docs.openclaw.ai/llms.txt): Full OpenClaw documentation map, including the ClawHub documentation section.
- [Markdown docs export](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub.md): Clean Markdown export of the ClawHub overview. Append `.md` to any docs page URL for the Markdown version.
- [OpenAPI v1](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/openapi.json): Machine-readable API schema for ClawHub v1 endpoints.

## Public Registry Surfaces

- [Skills](https://clawhub.ai/skills): Browse and search skill bundles centered on `SKILL.md`.
- [Plugins and packages](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Browse and search OpenClaw plugin/package records, including code plugins and bundle plugins.
- [Publishers](https://clawhub.ai/publishers): Browse user and organization publishers.
- [Skill publish](https://clawhub.ai/skills/publish): Web publishing flow for new skill versions.
- [Plugin/package publish](https://clawhub.ai/plugins/publish): Web publishing flow for plugin package records.
- [Search](https://clawhub.ai/search): Unified search entry point for public catalog discovery.

## Canonical URL Patterns

- [Skill page pattern](https://clawhub.ai/skills): Skill detail pages use `https://clawhub.ai/<owner>/<slug>` when an owner handle is available, with `/skills/<slug>` compatibility routes for existing unscoped links.
- [Skill security audit pattern](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/security-audits): Artifact audit pages use `/<owner>/<slug>/security-audit`.
- [Plugin/package page pattern](https://clawhub.ai/plugins): Plugin package detail pages use `/plugins/<name>` for unscoped packages and `/plugins/<scope>/<name>` for scoped packages.
- [Publisher page](https://clawhub.ai/publishers): Publisher profiles use public publisher routes and expose published artifacts.

## Documentation

- [ClawHub overview](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub): What ClawHub hosts, how OpenClaw and the ClawHub CLI differ, publishing, security, moderation, telemetry, and environment overrides.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/quickstart): Find, install, update, publish, sync, and inspect skills or plugins.
- [How ClawHub works](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/how-it-works): Registry records, listings, versions, installs, publishing, scans, moderation, and public API access.
- [CLI](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/cli): Complete `clawhub` CLI reference, global flags, config paths, auth, search, install, update, publish, transfer, package, and sync commands.
- [Publishing](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/publishing): Owner-scoped skill and plugin publishing, package scope rules, dry-run workflows, and reusable GitHub Actions.
- [Skill format](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/skill-format): `SKILL.md`, frontmatter metadata, runtime requirements, allowed files, size limits, slugs, versions, and tags.
- [Auth](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/auth): GitHub sign-in, API tokens, CLI login, device login, token storage, and revocation.
- [Telemetry](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/telemetry): Install telemetry sent by `clawhub sync`, opt-out behavior, and privacy expectations.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/troubleshooting): Fixes for sign-in, install, update, publish, sync, plugin install, and public API failures.
- [API v1](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/api): Public REST API overview, rate limits, errors, auth rules, public read endpoints, and write endpoints.
- [HTTP API](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/http-api): Detailed HTTP API reference for public, CLI, package, plugin, and auth routes.
- [Security audits](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/security-audits): How to interpret audit status, risk level, findings, SkillSpector, VirusTotal, and ClawScan results.
- [Moderation and account safety](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/moderation): Reports, moderation holds, hidden or blocked listings, bans, account standing, and publisher guidance.
- [Acceptable usage](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/acceptable-usage): Marketplace policy, allowed patterns, disallowed abuse workflows, reviewer notes, and enforcement.

## API References

- [Search skills](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/search?q=calendar): Search public skills by query, with relevance based on text match, embeddings, and small popularity priors.
- [List skills](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/skills): List public skills with pagination, sort options, and public filtering.
- Skill detail pattern: `https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/skills/<slug>` fetches one public skill by slug, including latest version, owner, metadata, and visible moderation state.
- Skill file pattern: `https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/skills/<slug>/file?path=SKILL.md` fetches a text file from a skill version.
- Skill download pattern: `https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/download?slug=<slug>` downloads a skill archive.
- [List packages](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/packages): List public package records across package families.
- [Search plugins](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/plugins/search?q=calendar): Search plugin package records.
- [Whoami](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/whoami): Verify a ClawHub API token with `Authorization: Bearer clh_...`.
- [OpenAPI v1](https://clawhub.ai/api/v1/openapi.json): Full endpoint schema and request/response shapes.

## CLI Workflows

- [Install ClawHub CLI](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/cli): Install with `npm i -g clawhub` or `pnpm add -g clawhub`.
- [Login](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/auth): Use `clawhub login`, `clawhub login --device`, or `clawhub login --token clh_...`.
- [Search and inspect skills](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/cli): Use `clawhub search`, `clawhub explore`, and `clawhub inspect <slug>`.
- [Install and update skills](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/quickstart): Prefer `openclaw skills install <slug>` for OpenClaw workspaces; direct `clawhub install <slug>` installs into `./skills`.
- [Publish skills](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/publishing): Use `clawhub sync --dry-run --owner <owner>`, then `clawhub sync --all --owner <owner>`, or publish one folder with `clawhub skill publish <path>`.
- [Publish packages](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/publishing): Use `clawhub package publish <source> --family code-plugin --dry-run`, then publish after confirming metadata, source attribution, compatibility, and artifact plan.
- [Inspect packages](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/cli): Use `clawhub package explore`, `clawhub package inspect <name>`, `clawhub package download`, `clawhub package verify`, and readiness or migration status commands.

## Source and Project Context

- [Source repository](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub): TanStack Start web app, Convex backend, ClawHub CLI, moderation CLI, schemas, public docs, and specs.
- [Public docs source](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/docs): Source for the ClawHub documentation mirrored into the OpenClaw docs site.
- [Schema package](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/packages/schema): Shared API route constants, types, package schemas, text-file rules, license rules, and OpenClaw compatibility contracts.
- [ClawHub CLI package](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/packages/clawhub): CLI implementation for auth, install, update, inspect, publish, packages, and sync.
- [Convex backend](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/convex): Database schema, HTTP API handlers, auth, publishing, search, downloads, telemetry, security scans, moderation, packages, plugins, and publishers.

## Optional

- [robots.txt](https://clawhub.ai/robots.txt): Crawler policy for the ClawHub site.
- [Manifest](https://clawhub.ai/manifest.json): Web app manifest.
- [Repository README](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub#readme): Project overview and local development map.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contributor setup and validation guidance.
